title: Lists and grouped content

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Lists and grouped content; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Lists and grouped content
sources.claim: Ordered lists communicate sequence, unordered lists communicate a set without ranking, and description lists associate terms with descriptions.

sources.claim: Ordered lists communicate sequence, unordered lists communicate a set without ranking, and description lists associate terms with descriptions.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Ordered lists communicate sequence, unordered lists communicate a set without ranking, and description lists associate terms with descriptions.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Recipe steps use ol because changing order changes the result, while ingredient names use ul. Your goal is to select and create the appropriate list structure for steps, sets and term-value pairs. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Standards-mode document skeleton; Paragraph and inline meaning. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Ordered lists communicate sequence, unordered lists communicate a set without ranking, and description lists associate terms with descriptions. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Visual bullets alone do not create list semantics, and navigation menus are usually lists of links rather than arbitrary div rows. This draft therefore rejects: All groups of repeated text should use ul because list order never affects meaning.

includes: Use ordered, unordered and description lists according to content relationships.
Select and create the appropriate list structure for steps, sets and term-value pairs.
excludes: Visual bullets alone do not create list semantics, and navigation menus are usually lists of links rather than arbitrary div rows.

outcomes: Select and create the appropriate list structure for steps, sets and term-value pairs.

walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: Recipe steps use ol because changing order changes the result, while ingredient names use ul.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Choose ol when sequence is meaningful, ul when it is not, and preserve real list elements instead of visual imitation.
